title: MULTNOMAH COUNTY CRISIS LINE - 211info
text: MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MULTNOMAH COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports people in crisis with mental health issues, suicidal thoughts, or emergencies. Provides phone counseling, guidance on next steps, and can dispatch a mobile team or arrange transport to a care facility. Also offers referrals to mental health and addiction treatment services. Also serves as the front door to Multnomah Behavioral Health Services (mental health and substance use disorder), the behavioral health benefit for Oregon Health Plan members in Multnomah County assigned to Health Share of Oregon CCO. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 520 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 988-5887 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Administration (503) 988-5887 Member Services Cheryl Lemley Toll-free (800) 716-9769 Call Center Helpline (503) 988-4888 Call Center Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for crisis counseling or information and referral regarding behavioral health services OHP members whose mental health insurance provider is Multnomah Behavioral Health, may go to any of the provider agencies listed on the county website and in the member handbook. Those in need of help choosing a provider agency, or who did not receive a member handbook, may call Multnomah Behavioral Health Member Services for a referral. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines In Person Crisis Intervention Mental Health Crisis Lines Suicide Prevention Hotlines Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ADULT PROTECTIVE SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - PATH If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH
